pages.raw_text:
Eighteen deeds were filed with
the county auditors office at the
close of business yesterday evening
by representatives of the Hill lines,
showing that the Gail Dray and
other purchases of Front avenue
property, made a few years ago,
were for the Great Northern. The
several pieces of property were
purchased in the names of Gail
Dray, Fred H. Rawson and F. w.
Wilk, who in turn conveyed the
ground to L. w. Hill, president of
the Great Northern, who thereafter
cleared the title by deciding the
land over to the Great Northern.
In the deals, made four years ago,
is involved $233,967. The property
is much more valuable today.


The filing of these deeds at this
time is taken as notice from the
clear of East Front avenue. The
Mr.. Walsh of Chicago,
who made the recent purchases
running to between 100000 and
$500, 900, is looked upon as another
agent of the willy Mr.. Hill, although
he has repeatedly denied that he is
so Connected.


With the Gail Dray tracts in the
hands of the Great Northern it is
taken for granted that no other
line would undertake to buy in at
that point, as the effort to acquire
terminal facilities there would be
useless. r~.ss ,.4-- ~. .s.sSsS (
